title: Peda

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: ½ cup condensed milk (sweet and creamy)
¼ tsp cardamom powder (green cardamom for authentic flavor)
A few strands of saffron (optional, soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk)

instruction.title: Prepare the Pan
steps: Heat a non-stick pan on low flame. Add ghee and let it melt.

instruction.title: Combine Ingredients
steps: Add milk powder and condensed milk to the pan. Mix well with a spatula.

instruction.title: Cook the Mixture
steps: Keep stirring continuously on low flame to avoid burning. Cook until the mixture thickens and starts leaving the sides of the pan (~8–10 minutes).

instruction.title: Flavor the Pedas
steps: Add cardamom powder and saffron milk. Mix thoroughly. Cook for another 1–2 minutes. The mixture should form a soft, pliable dough.

instruction.title: Shape the Pedas
steps: Remove from heat and let it cool slightly until warm to touch. Grease your hands with ghee and shape into small round discs or slightly flattened balls.

instruction.title: Garnish
steps: Press a small piece of almond or pistachio on top of each peda.

instruction.title: Set & Serve
steps: Let the pedas cool completely. Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5–6 days or in the refrigerator for longer shelf life.

chefTips: Use full-fat milk powder for the creamy texture; skimmed milk powder gives a dry texture.
Constant stirring is crucial; avoid high heat to prevent burning or graininess.
Slightly undercook if you prefer very soft, melt-in-mouth pedas.
For an exotic flavor, you can add a drop of rose water or kewra essence.
